_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how to use full stops, question marks and exclamation marks in our writing, and how to use capital letters correctly.

_source.w: We are learning how to use full stops, question marks and exclamation marks in our writing, and how to use capital letters correctly.
y: Punctuation guides readers through our writing, telling them when to pause or change tone.
Using the right punctuation makes our writing clearer and easier to understand.
Capital letters and punctuation are part of writing correctly in English.
eg: We put a full stop at the end of every telling sentence.
We use a question mark when we write a sentence that asks something.
We add an exclamation mark when a sentence shows strong feeling — like 'Wow!'
We start every sentence with a capital letter.
We use capital letters for people's names and the word 'I'.
We proofread a text and fix any missing or wrong punctuation marks.
